extensions.name: ShadyPanda Phase 4 spyware (Edge)
extensions.notes: ShadyPanda Phase 4 Edge spyware. Publisher Starlab Technology. WeTab flagship has 3M installs. Collects every URL, search query, mouse click (pixel XY coords). Transmits to 17 domains (8 Baidu servers China, 7 WeTab servers China, Google Analytics). 4M+ combined users. Still live in Edge store at disclosure Dec 2025.

extensions.name: WAME
extensions.notes: DBX Tecnologia / Grupo OPT WhatsApp automation campaign (Socket, October 2025). 131-extension franchise-model spamware operation targeting WhatsApp Web users. Brazilian company DBX Tecnologia licensed white-label builds to resellers under suporte@grupoopt.com.br and kaio.feitosa@grupoopt.com.br. Stage 5A static analysis by TPCI (May 2026) additionally identified midia.wascript.com.br data harvesting C2 infrastructure and confirmed form harvesting, cookie access, and credential theft behavior beyond the bulk messaging activity documented by Socket. Source: socket.dev/blog/131-spamware-extensions-targeting-whatsapp-flood-chrome-web-store | Original note: Stub entry imported from malicious_extension_sentry (MIT license). Source: https://github.com/toborrm9/malicious_extension_sentry — Name, campaign, threat type and date pending enrichment.

extensions.name: Click & Pick
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. The Netflix Party cluster (previously flagged by McAfee) spies on browsing history by sending full page URLs with unique user IDs to remote servers and performs affiliate fraud and cookie stuffing via dynamic declarativeNetRequest rule injection and hidden iframe injection. 20 users.

extensions.name: Wistia Video Downloader
extensions.notes: GitLab TamperedChef campaign. Threat actor assessed to have PURCHASED extensions from developers (ownership-transfer vector). Stripped CSP headers from first 2000 sites per session. Facilitated ad/SEO fraud. Last malicious update: 2024-08-08. Reported to Google Jan 2025, removed Feb 2025.
Stub entry imported from malicious_extension_sentry (MIT license). Source: https://github.com/toborrm9/malicious_extension_sentry — Name, campaign, threat type and date pending enrichment.

extensions.name: DarkSpectre ShadyPanda expansion / Zoom Stealer (name unconfirmed)
extensions.notes: DarkSpectre (China-attributed) ShadyPanda expansion cluster. 100+ extensions connected to same infinitynewtab.com/jt2x.com infrastructure. Mix of Zoom Stealer (meeting intelligence harvesting from 28+ conferencing platforms via WebSocket) and ShadyPanda affiliate fraud/surveillance. Individual extension names unconfirmed — ID is the stable identifier.
Stub entry imported from malicious_extension_sentry (MIT license). Source: https://github.com/toborrm9/malicious_extension_sentry — Name, campaign, threat type and date pending enrichment.

extensions.name: styleflex (Dormant Colors campaign)
extensions.notes: Dormant Colors campaign (Guardio Labs). 30 color-themed extensions side-loaded malicious code post-install. Hijacked search results, injected affiliate links to 10,000+ sites. 1M+ installs combined. This is the lead extension (styleflex) confirmed in Guardio report. 29 other campaign IDs not publicly indexed — only icons/names published by Guardio.
Source: https://github.com/chartingshow/crypto-firewall/issues/1554 — metadata pending

extensions.name: Adblocker for Chrome - NoAds
extensions.notes: GitLab TamperedChef campaign. Threat actor assessed to have PURCHASED extensions from developers (ownership-transfer vector). Stripped CSP headers from first 2000 sites per session. Facilitated ad/SEO fraud. Last malicious update: 2024-08-22. Reported to Google Jan 2025, removed Feb 2025.

extensions.name: "Bugatti Vs Lamborghini Wallpaper HD New Tab"
AdBlock for Youtube: Skip-n-Watch
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. The Netflix Party cluster (previously flagged by McAfee) spies on browsing history by sending full page URLs with unique user IDs to remote servers and performs affiliate fraud and cookie stuffing via dynamic declarativeNetRequest rule injection and hidden iframe injection. 3,000 users.

extensions.name: Clean Master (ShadyPanda Phase 3 RCE)
extensions.notes: ShadyPanda Phase 3 RCE backdoor. Extensions operated cleanly 2018-2019 through mid-2024, then weaponized via silent update. Hourly RCE: checks api.extensionplay[.]com, executes arbitrary JS with full browser API access. Exfiltrates AES-encrypted browsing history to cleanmasters[.]store. Anti-analysis: detects dev tools. 300k Chrome users. Google removed extensions.

extensions.name: Dopni - Automatic Cashback Service
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. extensions.name: Convert PDF to JPEG/PNG
extensions.notes: adindex ad fraud campaign (Palant Feb 2025). 20,000 users. Ownership transfer 2024 followed by privilege expansion Sep 2024. Part of cluster sharing malicious remote code execution functionality. Downloads JS payload from Firebase/internetdownloadmanager.top, disables CSP on all pages, replays recorded browsing sessions to commit ad fraud for adindex advertisers.

extensions.name: Browser game extension (malicious, name unconfirmed)
extensions.notes: Browser game extensions abusing broad host permissions. Palant Jun 2023 series. Games used as cover to gain all-website access for data collection/affiliate fraud.

extensions.name: Smart Auto Refresh
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. The Netflix Party cluster (previously flagged by McAfee) spies on browsing history by sending full page URLs with unique user IDs to remote servers and performs affiliate fraud and cookie stuffing via dynamic declarativeNetRequest rule injection and hidden iframe injection. 100,000 users.

extensions.name: Youtube Unblocked
extensions.notes: RedDirection / Koi Security Jul 2025 campaign. Edge browser extensions. Sleeper agents that activate browser hijacking on each page navigation: capture URL, send to C2 with unique user ID, receive redirect instructions. C2 domains: admitab.com, edmitab.com, click.videocontrolls.com, etc. 2.3M total users across Chrome+Edge. Most removed from stores.

extensions.name: CBS Full Episodes & Live TV (fake)
extensions.notes: Krebs/Nguyen May 2021 fake brand extension network. 45 extensions spoofed Adobe, Amazon, CBS, FITE, HBO, IMVU, Microsoft, Ring, Roku. Prompted users for personal/financial data. Network of fake Google reviewer accounts used to boost credibility. ~100k combined downloads. All removed by Google.

extensions.notes: adindex ad fraud campaign (Palant Feb 2025). 70,000 users. Featured. Primary subject of analysis. Ownership transfer 2024. extensions.name: Mike Adblock fuer Chrome
extensions.notes: GitLab TamperedChef campaign. Threat actor assessed to have PURCHASED extensions from developers (ownership-transfer vector). Stripped CSP headers from first 2000 sites per session. Facilitated ad/SEO fraud. Last malicious update: 2024-07-18. Reported to Google Jan 2025, removed Feb 2025.

extensions.name: Autoskip for Youtube™ Ads
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. The Netflix Party cluster (previously flagged by McAfee) spies on browsing history by sending full page URLs with unique user IDs to remote servers and performs affiliate fraud and cookie stuffing via dynamic declarativeNetRequest rule injection and hidden iframe injection. 200,000 users.
Stub entry imported from malicious_extension_sentry (MIT license). Source: https://github.com/toborrm9/malicious_extension_sentry — Name, campaign, threat type and date pending enrichment.

extensions.name: Easy Dark Mode
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. The Netflix Party cluster (previously flagged by McAfee) spies on browsing history by sending full page URLs with unique user IDs to remote servers and performs affiliate fraud and cookie stuffing via dynamic declarativeNetRequest rule injection and hidden iframe injection. 869 users.
“These extensions have not all been confirmed to be malicious by other third-party researchers.  EXT-NAME was a best effort and could be wrong.”

extensions.name: Christmas Eve - Slot Machine
extensions.notes: Socket April 2026 MaaS campaign. C2 infrastructure at cloudapi[.]stream. Steals Google OAuth identity and session data. Part of 108-extension coordinated campaign identified by Socket Threat Research Team (socket.dev, Apr 2026). Stage 5A confirmed. | Original note: Source: https://github.com/chartingshow/crypto-firewall/issues/1554 — metadata pending

extensions.name: ShadyPanda Phase 3 RCE extension
extensions.notes: ShadyPanda Phase 3 RCE backdoor. Extensions operated cleanly 2018-2019 through mid-2024, then weaponized via silent update. Hourly RCE: checks api.extensionplay[.]com, executes arbitrary JS with full browser API access. Exfiltrates AES-encrypted browsing history to cleanmasters[.]store. Anti-analysis: detects dev tools. 300k Chrome users. Google removed extensions.
Stub entry imported from malicious_extension_sentry (MIT license). Source: https://github.com/toborrm9/malicious_extension_sentry — Name, campaign, threat type and date pending enrichment.

extensions.notes: DBX Tecnologia / Grupo OPT WhatsApp automation campaign (Socket, October 2025). 131-extension franchise-model spamware operation targeting WhatsApp Web users. Brazilian company DBX Tecnologia licensed white-label builds to resellers under suporte@grupoopt.com.br and kaio.feitosa@grupoopt.com.br. Stage 5A static analysis by TPCI (May 2026) additionally identified midia.wascript.com.br data harvesting C2 infrastructure and confirmed form harvesting, cookie access, and credential theft behavior beyond the bulk messaging activity documented by Socket. extensions.name: Smart Adblocker
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. The Netflix Party cluster (previously flagged by McAfee) spies on browsing history by sending full page URLs with unique user IDs to remote servers and performs affiliate fraud and cookie stuffing via dynamic declarativeNetRequest rule injection and hidden iframe injection. 50,000 users.

extensions.name: ShadyPanda Phase 3 RCE extension
extensions.notes: ShadyPanda Phase 3 RCE backdoor. Extensions operated cleanly 2018-2019 through mid-2024, then weaponized via silent update. Hourly RCE: checks api.extensionplay[.]com, executes arbitrary JS with full browser API access. Exfiltrates AES-encrypted browsing history to cleanmasters[.]store. Anti-analysis: detects dev tools. 300k Chrome users. Google removed extensions.

Brazilian company DBX Tecnologia licensed white-label builds to resellers under suporte@grupoopt.com.br and kaio.feitosa@grupoopt.com.br. Stage 5A static analysis by TPCI (May 2026) additionally identified midia.wascript.com.br data harvesting C2 infrastructure and confirmed form harvesting, cookie access, and credential theft behavior beyond the bulk messaging activity documented by Socket. Source: socket.dev/blog/131-spamware-extensions-targeting-whatsapp-flood-chrome-web-store | Original note: Stub entry imported from malicious_extension_sentry (MIT license). Source: https://github.com/toborrm9/malicious_extension_sentry — Name, campaign, threat type and date pending enrichment.

extensions.name: Unlock Discord - VPN Proxy to Unblock Discord Anywhere
extensions.notes: RedDirection campaign (Koi Security, Jul 2025). Extensions stayed clean for years then received silent malicious updates. C2 backdoor tracked every URL visited. OWNERSHIP-TRANSFER=1: extensions acquired legitimately then weaponized. 2.3M users affected across Chrome+Edge. Reported to Google+Microsoft Jul 2025.

extensions.name: ShadyPanda affiliate fraud extension
extensions.notes: ShadyPanda Phase 1/2 affiliate fraud + search hijacking. Injected affiliate codes on eBay/Amazon/Booking.com clicks. Phase 2 extensions hijacked searches via trovi.com, exfiltrated cookies/search queries. Publisher nuggetsno15 (Chrome). 7-year campaign by China-attributed actor.

extensions.name: Volume Booster
extensions.notes: RedDirection / Koi Security Jul 2025 campaign. Edge browser extensions. Sleeper agents that activate browser hijacking on each page navigation: capture URL, send to C2 with unique user ID, receive redirect instructions. C2 domains: admitab.com, edmitab.com, click.videocontrolls.com, etc. 2.3M total users across Chrome+Edge. Most removed from stores.

extensions.name: Free adblocker
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. The Netflix Party cluster (previously flagged by McAfee) spies on browsing history by sending full page URLs with unique user IDs to remote servers and performs affiliate fraud and cookie stuffing via dynamic declarativeNetRequest rule injection and hidden iframe injection. 8,000 users.

extensions.name: Ultralight Photo Editor (fake)
extensions.notes: Krebs/Nguyen May 2021 fake brand extension network. 45 extensions spoofed Adobe, Amazon, CBS, FITE, HBO, IMVU, Microsoft, Ring, Roku. Prompted users for personal/financial data. Network of fake Google reviewer accounts used to boost credibility. ~100k combined downloads. All removed by Google.

extensions.name: DragonChat
extensions.notes: DBX Tecnologia / Grupo OPT WhatsApp automation campaign (Socket, October 2025). 131-extension franchise-model spamware operation targeting WhatsApp Web users. Brazilian company DBX Tecnologia licensed white-label builds to resellers under suporte@grupoopt.com.br and kaio.feitosa@grupoopt.com.br. Stage 5A static analysis by TPCI (May 2026) additionally identified midia.wascript.com.br data harvesting C2 infrastructure and confirmed form harvesting, cookie access, and credential theft behavior beyond the bulk messaging activity documented by Socket. Source: socket.dev/blog/131-spamware-extensions-targeting-whatsapp-flood-chrome-web-store | Original note: Stub entry imported from malicious_extension_sentry (MIT license). Source: https://github.com/toborrm9/malicious_extension_sentry — Name, campaign, threat type and date pending enrichment.

extensions.name: ShadyPanda Phase 4 spyware (Edge)
extensions.notes: ShadyPanda Phase 4 Edge spyware. Publisher Starlab Technology. WeTab flagship has 3M installs. Collects every URL, search query, mouse click (pixel XY coords). Transmits to 17 domains (8 Baidu servers China, 7 WeTab servers China, Google Analytics). 4M+ combined users. Still live in Edge store at disclosure Dec 2025.

extensions.name: "Final Fantasy Wallpaper HD Custom New Tab"
Super dark mode
extensions.notes: GitLab TamperedChef campaign. Threat actor assessed to have PURCHASED extensions from developers (ownership-transfer vector). Stripped CSP headers from first 2000 sites per session. Facilitated ad/SEO fraud. Last malicious update: 2024-08-11. Reported to Google Jan 2025, removed Feb 2025.

extensions.name: ShadyPanda Phase 3 RCE extension
extensions.notes: ShadyPanda Phase 3 RCE backdoor. Extensions operated cleanly 2018-2019 through mid-2024, then weaponized via silent update. Anti-analysis: detects dev tools. 300k Chrome users. Google removed extensions.

extensions.name: Volume Booster - Super Sound Booster
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. The Netflix Party cluster (previously flagged by McAfee) spies on browsing history by sending full page URLs with unique user IDs to remote servers and performs affiliate fraud and cookie stuffing via dynamic declarativeNetRequest rule injection and hidden iframe injection. 8,000 users.

extensions.name: Font Expert: Identify Fonts from Images & Websites
extensions.notes: Two overlapping malicious extension clusters: Phoenix Invicta extensions circumvent Manifest V3 remote code restrictions by downloading server-side configurations to inject HTML/JS into web pages, strip CSP headers via declarativeNetRequest abuse, and inject hidden ad frames over search results. The Netflix Party cluster (previously flagged by McAfee) spies on browsing history by sending full page URLs with unique user IDs to remote servers and performs affiliate fraud and cookie stuffing via dynamic declarativeNetRequest rule injection and hidden iframe injection. 666 users.

extensions.notes: DBX Tecnologia / Grupo OPT WhatsApp automation campaign (Socket, October 2025). 131-extension franchise-model spamware operation targeting WhatsApp Web users. Brazilian company DBX Tecnologia licensed white-label builds to resellers under suporte@grupoopt.com.br and kaio.feitosa@grupoopt.com.br. Stage 5A static analysis by TPCI (May 2026) additionally identified midia.wascript.com.br data harvesting C2 infrastructure and confirmed form harvesting, cookie access, and credential theft behavior beyond the bulk messaging activity documented by Socket.
